post.description:
Handle Telnyx Call Control webhooks.

This endpoint processes all Telnyx events:
- call.initiated: Answer call and set up streaming
- call.answered: Send webhook status update
- call.hangup: Clean up resources
- call.recording.saved: Download and store recording
- message.*: Delegate to SMS pipeline

post.description:
Generate TwiML for incoming Twilio calls.

This endpoint is called by Twilio when a call comes in. It uses the
unified webhook processing architecture:
1. Parse and validate webhook
2. Retrieve metadata for outbound calls
3. Process call using CallWebhookProcessor
4. Build and return TwiML response

Handle Twilio recording status callbacks.

This endpoint is called by Twilio when a recording is completed or failed.
Uses the unified webhook processing architecture for consistent handling.

Called by Twilio when the agent picks up during a warm transfer.

Query params:
    conference: Conference room name to join
    summary: LLM-generated summary of the conversation to read to the agent
    caller_call_sid: Caller's Call SID (needed for agent_confirmation flow)
    agent_confirmation: 'true' to ask agent before connecting, 'false' to connect immediately
    unavailable_message: Message to play to caller if agent declines

Called by Twilio after the agent responds to the confirmation prompt.

Query params:
    conference: Conference room name
    caller_call_sid: Caller's Call SID to either connect or hang up
    unavailable_message: Message to play to caller on decline
    assistant_id: Assistant ID for LLM classification of speech

Form body (from <Gather>):
    Digits: DTMF digit pressed (1=yes, 2=no)
    SpeechResult: Speech-to-text result

Called by Twilio's <Record action="..."> immediately when the recording ends.
Downloads the recording, transcribes via Deepgram, sends SMS to agent.
Returns empty TwiML to hang up.

Status callback for the agent outbound call during a warm transfer.

Called by Twilio when the agent call ends, fails, or is not answered.
If the agent didn't pick up (no-answer / busy / failed), ends the caller's call.

Query params:
    caller_call_sid: The caller's Call SID to hang up on no-answer

post.description:
Send an SMS message through an assistant.

This endpoint allows sending SMS messages using the telephony provider
associated with the assistant that owns the from_phone_number.

The system will automatically:
1. Find the assistant associated with the from_phone_number
2. Use the assistant's configured telephony provider (Twilio, Telnyx, or Vonage)
3. Queue the SMS for delivery with per-provider rate limiting (default)
   or send immediately if queue=False
4. Persist the outbound message to the SMS conversation (ChatMessage) for UI display

Idempotency:
    Pass an Idempotency-Key header (or idempotency_key in body) to dedupe sends
    from retried requests. Format: e.g., "chat:123:msg:456:outbound"

Args:
    sms_data: SMS request containing from/to numbers, message, and queue option

Returns:
    SendSMSResponse: Success status, message ID, status, and provider info

patch.description:
Partially update an assistant (PATCH).

This endpoint allows selective updates to specific assistant fields without
requiring the full configuration. Only the fields provided will be updated,
all other fields remain unchanged.

For nested JSON fields (llm_settings, stt_settings, etc.), the provided
data is MERGED with existing data, not replaced. This means you can update
just one field within a settings object without losing the others.

Key difference from PUT:
- PATCH: Send only the fields you want to change (with smart merging)
- PUT: Requires handling the full assistant configuration

Supported fields:
- Basic: name, description, is_active
- LLM: llm_provider, llm_provider_config, custom_llm_url, llm_settings
- TTS/STT: tts_settings, stt_settings
- Webhooks: webhook_url, sms_webhook_url
- Messages: end_call_message, transfer_call_message, idle_message
- Settings: interruption_settings, tools_settings, rag_settings
- Custom: custom_settings (for structured_data_schema, etc.)

Example: If stt_settings has {model: "nova-2", language: "en"} and you
send {keyterms: "pizza pasta"}, the result will be {model: "nova-2",
language: "en", keyterms: "pizza pasta"}.

- Test LLM variance on the same call
- Update metrics after algorithm improvements

delete.description:
Archive an SMS conversation and purge all associated SMS data.

This does NOT delete the underlying Call record (which may be used for call logs).
Instead, it:
1. Purges all ChatMessage records for this conversation
2. Purges all SMSAuditLog records (debug logs)
3. Purges all SMSMessageLog records (message logs)
4. Marks the Call record as archived via call_meta['sms_deleted'] = True
5. Clears Redis session keys so future messages create a fresh conversation

After deletion, the conversation will no longer appear in the SMS UI, and
new messages from the same phone numbers will start a fresh conversation.

schema.title: Response Delete Sms Conversation Api V1 Sms Conversations  Conversation Id  Delete

- stt: True means Burki provides Deepgram keys
- carrier: True means Burki provides Vonage/Twilio keys

patch.description:
Update managed services configuration (Hybrid Mode).

Allows users to selectively use Burki-managed credentials for some services
while bringing their own API keys for others.

- Setting a service to True: Burki provides credentials (billed as pass-through + 15%)
- Setting a service to False: User provides their own API keys (BYO, no pass-through billing)

Only the services specified in the request will be updated. Other services
remain unchanged.

**Important:** If you set a service to False (BYO), make sure you have configured
your own API keys in the organization settings before making calls.

**Billing Impact:**
- Managed services: Pass-through cost + 15% management fee
- BYO services: No pass-through billing (you pay the provider directly)
- Platform fee ($0.03/min voice) always applies regardless of mode

post.description:
Handle Stripe webhook events.

This endpoint receives events from Stripe when payments are processed.
It verifies the signature to ensure authenticity and then processes
the event accordingly.

Key events handled:
- payment_intent.succeeded: Add funds to wallet
- payment_intent.payment_failed: Log the failure
- checkout.session.completed: Alternative payment flow

STTEndpointingSettings.title: STTEndpointingSettings
STTEndpointingSettings.description:
Endpointing settings for Speech-to-Text (STT).

- `silence_threshold` (ms): Deepgram endpointing window — passed to the
  Deepgram WebSocket as `endpointing`.
- `min_silence_duration` (ms): legacy utterance-timeout fallback used by
  `app/core/call_handler.py` when computing how long to wait after the
  last `is_final` before finalizing. Newer pipelines prefer
  `utterance_timeout_seconds`. Kept here so existing assistant rows
  continue to work; removed from the UI because users should configure
  utterance timeout via `utterance_timeout_seconds` instead.

friendly_name.description: Friendly name for the phone number

assistant_id.description: Assistant to assign the number to (null to unassign)

flow_id.title: Flow Id
flow_id.description: ConversationFlow to assign the number to (null to unassign)

sip_trunk_id.title: Sip Trunk Id

allowed_country_codes.title: Allowed Country Codes
allowed_country_codes.description: List of ISO country codes (e.g., ['US', 'CA', 'GB']) that are allowed to call this number. If None or empty, all countries are allowed.
